mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies aggregate demand shocks and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year windows, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production relative to potential output signals supply-side capacity utilization peaking, which historically precedes margin compression and inventory drawdowns, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in over 65% of triggered instances, or the signal's information coefficient fails to exceed 0.05 in any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth confirms underlying economic momentum, which supports earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon without requiring additional macro thresholds.
failure_condition: The sign of GDPC1 growth fails to predict the sign of 5-day forward SPX returns above 50% accuracy over a 10-year backtest, or the mean forward return in positive growth quarters does not exceed the mean in negative growth quarters by at least 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 218.5, inflation expectations force the Federal Reserve to tighten policy prematurely, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses the threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent movement where total nonfarm payrolls accelerate while the unemployment rate declines simultaneously indicates a structural labor market recovery, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to produce a statistically significant positive coefficient (p > 0.05) in a panel regression predicting 20-day SPX returns over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts combined with rising real GDP growth signals robust fixed investment and consumption, which historically leads equity markets higher over a 5-day horizon as earnings estimates are revised upward.
failure_condition: Forward SPX returns are negative or flat in over 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% when tested on out-of-sample data from 2008 to 2015.
